reasoning:
Lilly King has held the 100m breaststroke long-course world record since July 2017. As of the knowledge cutoff (October 26, 2025), Wikipedia shows she is still the record holder and there are no pending (#) records unratified for this event. 

Because the forecast spans the late autumn and winter seasons (November to January), professional swimming predominantly focuses on short-course (25m pool) events during this time. The long-course World Championships already occurred in August 2025 in Singapore, and the next peak long-course events (like National Trials or the next World Championships) will not occur until spring or summer of 2026. Given the absence of major long-course peaking events in this 3-month window and the fact that the record has stood unbroken for over 8 years, the probability of it being broken before late January 2026 is extremely low. Probabilities are set to the maximum allowed or near-maximum, reflecting the very short forecasting window and the seasonal nature of the sport.
